title: Gentle Silky Argentinian Seitan Stew
description: A comforting and silky Argentinian stew featuring seitan and celery salt, made with plant-based ingredients and a mortar-pestle-pound technique for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the seitan and cook until it is lightly browned on all sides.
Add the celery salt, vegetable broth, and tomato paste, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Use a mortar and pestle to pound the celery salt and mix it into the stew.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
